title: DuckDB Produces a CSV it cannot read when single quote column is present
description:
### What happens?

We can get DuckDB to produce a CSV which it cannot read with the same settings as the writer. ON write,

* we write one test column of each type with mock data
* `|` as a delimiter, default newline, header on, and `"` as the escape
* on read with the exact same configurations, we get an `Invalid Input Error:` that indicates that DuckDB read the CSV as being one line, even though we have `new_line = \n (Set By User)` and the generated csv properly has `\n` where expected
* this goes away if we omit `chr(39)` from test_unicode, which makes little sense because `'` is neither the escape, delimiter, or newline character. 


### To Reproduce

```sql
INSTALL SPATIAL;
LOAD SPATIAL;

COPY (
	SELECT * FROM (
		SELECT
			md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)) as id,
			(to_timestamp(range))::timestamptz as updated_at,
			rpad('hello_', CAST(range AS INTEGER), '0') as test_string,
			chr(CAST(range AS INTEGER)) as test_unicode,
			CAST(to_timestamp(range)::timestamp AS TIME) as test_time,
			CAST(md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)) AS BLOB) as test_bytes,
			CAST(range % 32767 AS SMALLINT) as test_smallint,
			CAST(range AS INTEGER) as test_integer,
			CAST(range AS BIGINT) as test_bigint,
			CAST(range / 3.0 AS REAL) as test_real,
			CAST(range / 3.0 AS DOUBLE) as test_double,
			CAST(range AS DECIMAL) as test_decimal,
			CASE WHEN range % 2 = 0 THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS test_boolean,
			CAST(DATE '2022-02-24' - TO_DAYS(CAST(range % 3650 AS INTEGER)) AS DATE) as test_date,
			json_object('number', range, 'string', md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)))::TEXT as test_json,
			ST_GeomFromGeoJSON(json_object('type', 'Point', 'coordinates', [CAST( 180.0 / (range + 1.0) AS DOUBLE), CAST(90.0 / (range + 1.0) AS DOUBLE)]::FLOAT[2])::STRING) as test_geography,
			[CAST(range / 1.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 2.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 3.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 4.0 AS DOUBLE)]::DOUBLE[4] as test_vector,
			CASE range % 6 WHEN 0 THEN 'madison' WHEN 1 THEN 'boston' WHEN 2 THEN 'redmond' WHEN 3 THEN 'sacramento' WHEN 4 THEN 'anchorage' WHEN 5 THEN 'reno' END as tenant_id
		FROM range(10000)
	) generated
	ORDER BY updated_at ASC LIMIT 10000) TO 'fails.csv' (HEADER TRUE, DELIMITER '|', ESCAPE '"');

SELECT * FROM read_csv('fails.csv', header=TRUE, new_line='\n', delim='|', escape='"', strict_mode=FALSE, types={'id': 'text', 'updated_at': 'timestamptz', 'test_bytes': 'blob', 'test_string': 'text', 'test_unicode': 'text', 'test_smallint': 'integer', 'test_integer': 'integer', 'test_bigint': 'bigint', 'test_real': 'double', 'test_double': 'double', 'test_boolean': 'boolean', 'test_decimal': 'text', 'test_date': 'date', 'test_time': 'time', 'test_json': 'text', 'test_vector': 'text', 'test_geography': 'text', 'tenant_id': 'text'}) ORDER BY updated_at;



COPY (
	SELECT * FROM (
		SELECT
			md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)) as id,
			(to_timestamp(range))::timestamptz as updated_at,
			rpad('hello_', CAST(range AS INTEGER), '0') as test_string,
			CASE WHEN range = 39 THEN NULL ELSE chr(CAST(range AS INTEGER)) END as test_unicode,
			CAST(to_timestamp(range)::timestamp AS TIME) as test_time,
			CAST(md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)) AS BLOB) as test_bytes,
			CAST(range % 32767 AS SMALLINT) as test_smallint,
			CAST(range AS INTEGER) as test_integer,
			CAST(range AS BIGINT) as test_bigint,
			CAST(range / 3.0 AS REAL) as test_real,
			CAST(range / 3.0 AS DOUBLE) as test_double,
			CAST(range AS DECIMAL) as test_decimal,
			CASE WHEN range % 2 = 0 THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS test_boolean,
			CAST(DATE '2022-02-24' - TO_DAYS(CAST(range % 3650 AS INTEGER)) AS DATE) as test_date,
			json_object('number', range, 'string', md5(CAST(range AS TEXT)))::TEXT as test_json,
			ST_GeomFromGeoJSON(json_object('type', 'Point', 'coordinates', [CAST( 180.0 / (range + 1.0) AS DOUBLE), CAST(90.0 / (range + 1.0) AS DOUBLE)]::FLOAT[2])::STRING) as test_geography,
			[CAST(range / 1.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 2.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 3.0 AS DOUBLE), CAST(range / 4.0 AS DOUBLE)]::DOUBLE[4] as test_vector,
			CASE range % 6 WHEN 0 THEN 'madison' WHEN 1 THEN 'boston' WHEN 2 THEN 'redmond' WHEN 3 THEN 'sacramento' WHEN 4 THEN 'anchorage' WHEN 5 THEN 'reno' END as tenant_id
		FROM range(10000)
	) generated
	ORDER BY updated_at ASC LIMIT 10000) TO 'succeeds.csv' (HEADER TRUE, DELIMITER '|', ESCAPE '"');

SELECT * FROM read_csv('succeeds.csv', header=TRUE, new_line='\n', delim='|', escape='"', strict_mode=FALSE, types={'id': 'text', 'updated_at': 'timestamptz', 'test_bytes': 'blob', 'test_string': 'text', 'test_unicode': 'text', 'test_smallint': 'integer', 'test_integer': 'integer', 'test_bigint': 'bigint', 'test_real': 'double', 'test_double': 'double', 'test_boolean': 'boolean', 'test_decimal': 'text', 'test_date': 'date', 'test_time': 'time', 'test_json': 'text', 'test_vector': 'text', 'test_geography': 'text', 'tenant_id': 'text'}) ORDER BY updated_at;
```

### OS:

Linux AMD64, OSX ARM64

### DuckDB Version:

v1.4.1

### DuckDB Client:

CLI, Golang

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Niger Little-Poole

### Affiliation:

Prequel

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id VARCHAR, updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE, test_string VARCHAR, test_unicode VARCHAR, test_time TIME, test_bytes BLOB, test_smallint INTEGER, test_integer INTEGER, test_bigint BIGINT, test_real DOUBLE PRECISION, test_double DOUBLE PRECISION, test_decimal DECIMAL, test_boolean BOOLEAN, test_date DATE, test_json VARCHAR, test_vector VARCHAR, test_geography VARCHAR, tenant_id VARCHAR);
COPY (SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE test_unicode IS NOT NULL) TO 'valid_data.csv' WITH (FORMAT CSV, HEADER TRUE, DELIMITER '|', ESCAPE '"');

SELECT * FROM read_csv('valid_data.csv', header=TRUE, new_line='\n', delim='|', escape='"', strict_mode=FALSE, types={'id': 'VARCHAR', 'updated_at': 'TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE', 'test_bytes': 'BLOB', 'test_string': 'VARCHAR', 'test_unicode': 'VARCHAR', 'test_smallint': 'INTEGER', 'test_integer': 'INTEGER', 'test_bigint': 'BIGINT', 'test_real': 'DOUBLE PRECISION', 'test_double': 'DOUBLE PRECISION', 'test_boolean': 'BOOLEAN', 'test_decimal': 'VARCHAR', 'test_date': 'DATE', 'test_time': 'TIME', 'test_json': 'VARCHAR', 'test_vector': 'VARCHAR', 'test_geography': 'VARCHAR', 'tenant_id': 'VARCHAR'}) ORDER BY updated_at;
key_bug_features: CSV read failure with single quote in column data
